title: Imperial College Discovers New Senolytic Class Targeting GPX4 in Senescent Cells
description: Imperial College London researchers published in Nature Cell Biology that senescent cells survive heightened oxidative stress by upregulating GPX4, and that new chloroacetamide compounds selectively kill them via ferroptosis. Combining GPX4 inhibitors with cancer therapy cleared persistent senescent tumor cells in melanoma, prostate and ovarian models, suggesting a one-two punch strategy.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 Hits Best-in-Class hsCRP Reductions
description: BioAge reported positive Phase 1 SAD/MAD data for BGE-102, a brain-penetrant oral NLRP3 inhibitor, showing potential best-in-class hsCRP reductions in obese participants with elevated inflammation and a clean safety profile. A Phase 2 cardiovascular-risk trial reads out H2 2026, with a Phase 1b/2a in diabetic macular edema slated for mid-2026.

title: Insilico Medicine Forms Industry-First Longevity Board
description: Insilico Medicine (HKEX: 3696) announced the formation of the industry's first Longevity Board to oversee aging-research strategy across life models, biomarkers and aging-target identification. The move signals that the recently public AI-pharma is institutionalizing geroscience as a core platform direction alongside its 13 INDs.

title: AstraZeneca's Tozorakimab Sweeps Three Phase 3 COPD Trials
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 antibody tozorakimab hit primary endpoints in MIRANDA, completing a clean Phase 3 sweep alongside OBERON and TITANIA in COPD with reductions in moderate-to-severe exacerbations. The wins set up filings for a first-in-class biologic and validate inflammaging as a tractable target where rivals from Sanofi/Regeneron and Roche have stumbled.

title: Mesoblast Acquires CAR-MSC Platform to Sharpen Cell Targeting
description: Mesoblast secured exclusive worldwide rights to a Mayo Clinic CAR technology platform for genetically engineered mesenchymal stromal cells, aiming to make its therapies home to inflamed tissue more precisely. Near-term programs target ulcerative colitis and Crohn's, with CD19 CAR-MSCs planned for lupus nephritis and other B-cell autoimmune diseases.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets a Key Vulnerability in Ageing Cells
description: Gil and Tate groups identified chloroacetamide senolytics that exploit senescent cells' GPX4 dependence, triggering ferroptosis selectively. Published in Nature Cell Biology with combination data in cancer models.

title: Mesoblast Advances CAR Cell Therapy and Phase 3 Pain Trial
description: Mesoblast (ASX:MSB) completed enrollment in its 300-patient Phase 3 trial of rexlemestrocel-L for chronic low back pain and licensed Mayo Clinic CAR technology to upgrade MSC targeting in immune disorders.

title: INBRAIN Completes Enrollment of First-in-Human Graphene BCI Study
description: INBRAIN Neuroelectronics finished recruitment for its graphene cortical interface trial, with eight of ten patients treated surgically and no device-related adverse events. Final results expected later in 2026.

title: David Sinclair Says Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial Is Days Away
description: At Abundance360, Sinclair said the first human epigenetic reprogramming trial using three Yamanaka factors will dose a patient's eye to test reversal of vision loss within weeks. He framed 2026 as the year human age reversal moves from speculation to early clinical signal.

title: Longevity Biotech Reaches $3.74B in Q1 2026 Financing
description: Longevity.Technology data showed Q1 2026 longevity biotech investment hit $3.74B across 49 financings, up 56% year-over-year, concentrated in epigenetic reprogramming and mitochondrial health.

title: Longevity Market Funding Trends Show Outlier Concentration
description: Twelve-month longevity tracker shows 14 disclosed deals totaling $792.9M from just 13 companies, with 5 rounds above $50M capturing 81% of dollars. North America held 97.8% of disclosed capital.

title: Longevity Breakthroughs Roundup Week 17 — Energy Span, GSK, Seragon
description: Phil Newman and Nina Patrick's roundup covered energy span as a fatigue biomarker, GSK's $950M 35Pharma deal, January AI joining Medicare, OsteoBoost's $8M raise, Seragon's SRN-901 33% mouse lifespan boost, and Epicutis skin data.

title: New Market Pitch Longevity Tracker — 13 Unique Companies, $792.9M
description: Pure-play longevity equity rounds May 2025-April 2026 highlight Function Health $298M, Loyal $100M, NewLimit (twice), and Aeovian $55M as anchor deals.

title: Mesoblast CAR-Cell Tech Deal Aims to Sharpen MSC Targeting
description: Detailed coverage of Mesoblast's Mayo Clinic CAR platform license and how recognition tags could improve MSC homing to inflamed tissue in IBD and beyond.

title: Insilico Medicine Forms Longevity Board to Accelerate AI Aging R&D
description: HKEX-listed Insilico Medicine announced an industry-first Longevity Board overseeing life models, aging biomarkers and target identification, formalizing geroscience as a strategic platform pillar.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102 NLRP3 Inhibitor
description: Brain-penetrant oral NLRP3 inhibitor showed potential best-in-class hsCRP reductions in obese participants with no serious adverse events. Phase 2 cardiovascular-risk POC data due H2 2026.

title: AstraZeneca's Tozorakimab Wins Third Phase 3 COPD Trial
description: MIRANDA (NCT06040086) showed statistically significant reductions in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations versus placebo on top of standard inhaled care, completing a clean three-trial sweep for the IL-33 antibody.

title: Longevity Investors Conference Announces 2026 Speaker Lineup
description: Annual Longevity Investors Conference revealed a 2026 lineup including Dave Asprey, Steve Horvath, and Garri Zmudze, signaling continued capital interest as macro support holds.

title: AstraZeneca's Tozorakimab Phase 3 Sweep Reignites Anti-IL-33 Class
description: AZ's three-for-three Phase 3 COPD wins (OBERON, TITANIA, MIRANDA) revive an anti-IL-33 modality that previously stumbled with Sanofi/Regeneron's itepekimab and Roche's astegolimab.

title: Largest Rapamycin Trial Ever Conducted in Humans Now Live
description: University of Arizona's 720-participant Phase 3 of weekly low-dose rapamycin runs two years dosing plus a third year follow-up, with co-primary endpoints of frailty transition and IL-6 plus a deep biomarker panel including epigenetic clocks.

title: Bryan Johnson on Biological Age and Healthy Code
description: At BI's Long Play event, Johnson emphasized resting heart rate as an underrated longevity metric and warned founders against monk-mode burnout undermining healthspan.

title: Mesoblast Acquires CAR-MSC Platform Startup, Mayo Clinic License
description: Deal financed via ASX share issuance covers exclusive worldwide CAR-MSC rights with near-term focus on UC, Crohn's, and CD19 CAR-MSCs for lupus nephritis.

title: Dr. Abe Ho on Emerging Longevity Developments
description: Longevity physician walks through stem-cell rejuvenation, AI-accelerated drug discovery (AlphaFold solving 200M structures in weeks), and how preventive medicine reshapes the lifespan-healthspan equation.

title: Peter Attia: Research Worth Sharing — April 2026 Edition
description: Curated set covering paternal exercise reshaping offspring fitness via sperm microRNAs, mRNA COVID vaccines boosting cancer immunotherapy, gamma light/sound stimulation for Alzheimer's, and autonomic imbalance as a unifying aging theory.

title: 2026 Longevity Stack: Senolytics, Peptides, Reprogramming
description: Practical 2026 framework layers GLP-1/peptide signaling (strongest evidence), senolytics (emerging clinical proof), and partial reprogramming (preclinical) onto the hallmarks of aging.

title: Peter Attia Episode 386 — Aging Clocks: What They Measure and How They Work
description: Deep dive on epigenetic and proteomic aging clocks, the gap between chronological and biological age, and how clinically actionable these biomarkers really are in 2026.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026 Set for Breakout Year
description: DLT Chatbot projections converge on $8-9B for full-year 2026 longevity biotech investment, with $10B-plus possible if macro and clinical catalysts cooperate.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Rapamycin Healthy-Aging Trial
description: NIA-funded multi-phase study in 84 healthy adults aged 65-90 evaluates rapamycin and everolimus dosing, biomarkers, and short- and long-term outcomes to establish evidence-based geroscience guidance.

title: Bryan Johnson's Updated 2026 Supplement Stack
description: Updated Blueprint stack drops rapamycin, adds low-dose lithium and NDGA, and shifts NMN/NR to six days a week, citing personal biomarker tracking results.

title: Epigenetic Clock Pace Predicts Mortality in 24-Year InCHIANTI Study
description: Longitudinal analysis of the InCHIANTI cohort (Pei-Lun Kuo et al., Nature Aging 2026) shows that the rate at which epigenetic clocks accelerate predicts death risk independent of starting age, validating clocks as longevity-intervention endpoints.
